sourceMovie.title: The Fall
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Fall (2006) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tarsem Singh's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Fall clicked for you, The Curious Case of Benjamin Button (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Fall?
faq.answer: Try The Curious Case of Benjamin Button, City of Angels, Hector and the Search for Happiness—each shares DNA with The Fall while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Fall?
faq.answer: The Fall (2006) is best described as Adventure, Fantasy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Fall worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
